summary: Container-isolated personal AI agent with messaging channels and per-agent workspaces.
editor_note: The smart bet is shrinking personal agents until the security model is legible. NanoClaw exposes a Node host, per-agent Docker containers, per-session SQLite files, multi-channel skills, and Agent Vault credential injection. Unlike OpenClaw-style agents that sit on the host, it makes isolation the default product surface. The risk is whether implementation services pull it away from the open-source simplicity that made it spread.
